corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: In the final evaluations of the agent, after the end of training, Rainbow achieves a median score of 223% in the no-ops regime; in the human starts regime we measured a median score of 153%.
The deep reinforcement learning community has made several independent improvements to the DQN algorithm.
This paper examines six extensions to the DQN algorithm and empirically studies their combination.
Our experiments show that the combination provides state-of-the-art performance on the Atari 2600 benchmark, both in terms of data efficiency and final performance.
Our experiments show that the combination provides state-of-the-art performance on the Atari 2600 benchmark, both in terms of data efficiency and final performance.
The many recent successes in scaling reinforcement learning (RL) to complex sequential decision-making problems were kick-started by the Deep Q-Networks algorithm (DQN; .
The dueling network architecture helps to generalize across actions by separately representing state values and action advantages.
Curves are smoothed with a moving average over 5 points. radically different issues, and since they build on a shared framework, they could plausibly be combined.
In this paper we propose to study an agent that combines all the aforementioned ingredients.

In fact, their combination results in new stateof-the-art results on the benchmark suite of 57 Atari 2600 games from the Arcade Learning Environment , both in terms of data efficiency and of final performance.

Policies of this kind are used to introduce a form of exploration: by randomly selecting actions that are sub-optimal according to its current estimates, the agent can discover and correct its estimates when appropriate.
The main limitation is that it is difficult to discover alternative courses of action that extend far into the future; this has motivated research on more directed forms of exploration.
The main limitation is that it is difficult to discover alternative courses of action that extend far into the future; this has motivated research on more directed forms of exploration.
While we consider them to be important and complementary, we leave questions of scalability and parallelism to future work.
We have demonstrated that several improvements to DQN can be successfully integrated into a single learning algorithm that achieves state-of-the-art performance.
We have demonstrated that several improvements to DQN can be successfully integrated into a single learning algorithm that achieves state-of-the-art performance.
We have demonstrated that several improvements to DQN can be successfully integrated into a single learning algorithm that achieves state-of-the-art performance.
Moreover, we have shown that within the integrated algorithm, all but one of the components provided clear performance benefits.

To evaluate Rainbow fairly against the baselines, we have followed the common domain modifications of clipping rewards, fixed action-repetition, and frame-stacking, but these might be removed by other learning algorithm improvements.
